tiles.text: I have a horrible feeling that I'm a greedy, perverted, selfish, apathetic, cynical, depraved, morally bankrupt woman

groups.name: Fleabag Universe
groups.explanation: Fleabag (show title and protagonist nickname), Phoebe Waller-Bridge (writer/actor), The Priest (character played by Andrew Scott), and the self-flagellating monologue quote are all from the BBC series Fleabag.

groups.name: TRAP — Queens of Ruthless Ambition (But NOT All the Same Show)
groups.explanation: These tiles look like they all belong to Game of Thrones, but only Cersei Lannister and Lena Headey are directly from GoT. 'Stormborn' is a Daenerys Targaryen title from GoT, while 'Shame' is the infamous GoT walk-of-atonement chant — yet Lena Headey also starred in Terminator: The Sarah Connor Chronicles and The Purge TV series, making her the trap tile that bridges shows. The intended grouping is: tiles that are associated specifically with Cersei Lannister/Lena Headey's Game of Thrones storyline — Cersei Lannister (character), Stormborn (episode title in which Cersei's power is challenged), Shame (the septa's chant during Cersei's walk), and Lena Headey (actress who played Cersei).

groups.name: TV Antiheroes Who Address the Camera Directly
groups.explanation: Tony Soprano (The Sopranos) addresses the camera in dream sequences and asides; Nucky Thompson (Boardwalk Empire) in certain theatrical monologue moments; Jimmy McNulty (The Wire) occasionally winks at the absurdity of the system in near-direct address; and Frank Underwood (House of Cards) is one of TV's most famous fourth-wall-breakers. All are male antihero protagonists in prestige cable/streaming dramas who at least once speak directly to or acknowledge the audience.

groups.name: TV Bar/Hangout Spots Named After a Type of Wood
groups.explanation: Each tile is a fictional TV gathering place whose name contains or references a type of wood: Hinoki (Japanese cypress wood — the upscale bar in Succession Season 3 used for deal-making), Greendale Community College (GREENDALE contains 'alder' wood inside: greenDALE — and the show Community hinges on the study group room 'The Greendale Study Room'), Paddy's Pub (PADDY contains 'padauk' wood resonance — trap tile, actually from It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia), and The Bamboo (bamboo is a woody grass — the beach bar in Survivor: Pearl Islands, used as a recurring set). NOTE: This group is the hardest and most lateral — the intended connection is fictional TV locations whose names conceal or reference a type of timber/wood material.
